overview: Gruul Ramp in Pauper leverages cheap mana dorks and land acceleration to deploy large threats ahead of curve. By utilizing signature cards like Utopia Sprawl and Avenging Hunter, the deck generates massive mana advantage while applying early pressure. The goal is to outvalue opponents through card advantage and overwhelming board presence.
win_conditions: Deploying oversized creatures like Annoyed Altisaur ahead of curve
Generating massive mana with Utopia Sprawl and Avenging Hunter to play multiple threats per turn
Overwhelming the opponent with a wide board of mana-producing creatures that draw cards

keep_criteria: Hands with at least two mana sources by turn two
Early mana dorks like Avenging Hunter or Utopia Sprawl
A clear path to turn three or four threats like Annoyed Altisaur
Keep hands with 2-3 lands, at least one ramp spell, and a threat (Annoyed Altisaur or Avenging Hunter).
Keep hands with Utopia Sprawl if paired with a land or cheap creature.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with only high-cost threats and no ramp
Hands with multiple lands but no creatures
Mulligan hands with 4+ lands, no ramp, or only high-cost threats.

early_game: Play lands and cheap ramp creatures on curve
Prioritize Avenging Hunter and Utopia Sprawl to build mana base
Avoid overextending into removal without board presence
Turn 1: Play a land, optionally cast a cheap ramp spell if available.
Turn 2: Play a second land, ramp again or deploy a cheap threat.
Prioritize land drops to ensure you can cast your ramp spells on curve, aiming to hit turn 3-4 for your signature threats.
mid_game: Deploy Annoyed Altisaur and other mid-sized threats
Use excess mana to play multiple creatures per turn
Start applying pressure while continuing to ramp
Preserve mana for your big threats
Track graveyard if relevant, but focus on hand and library resources to ensure you can cast threats when they hit.
Commit threats when you have enough mana to protect them or close the game.
Hold back Annoyed Altisaur if the opponent has board wipes or targeted removal.
Use Avenging Hunter to stabilize or apply immediate pressure.
late_game: Flood the board with large creatures using accumulated mana
Use card draw from lands or creatures to find finishers
Close out the game with an unblockable army or massive threat
Annoyed Altisaur and Avenging Hunter, often boosted by Utopia Sprawl.
Combine evasion with unblockable effects to deal lethal damage.
Go all-in when you have enough mana to cast a threat and have a clear path to close the game, or when the opponent is out of answers.

key_cards.name: Annoyed Altisaur
key_cards.role: Mid-game threat and card advantage engine
key_cards.notes: Deploy on turn four or five to stabilize the board and draw cards, leveraging excess mana from ramp.

key_cards.name: Avenging Hunter
key_cards.role: Early mana dork and board presence
key_cards.notes: Play on turn two to accelerate mana and provide a 2/2 body that pressures opponents early.

key_cards.name: Utopia Sprawl
key_cards.notes: Tutor for basic lands to ensure consistent mana fixing and ramp, enabling faster deployment of threats.

sequencing_tips: Play Avenging Hunter before Utopia Sprawl to maximize early mana advantage
Hold up mana for counterspells or removal if the opponent plays interaction
Prioritize playing lands before creatures to avoid mana screw on turn three
Use excess mana to play multiple ramp creatures in a single turn to trigger synergies
Annoyed Altisaur cannot be blocked by creatures with power 4 or greater, making it a premier finisher.
Utopia Sprawl makes creatures unblockable by creatures with power 2 or less.
Watch out for board wipes and targeted removal.
Remember that Gruul Ramp is a race

matchups.advice: Prioritize early blockers and use ramp to outpace their curve
Apply early pressure with Avenging Hunter and use card draw to outlast their resources
Race them by deploying threats ahead of curve and leveraging mana advantage
Cast threats at end of turn, use multiple threats to force counter trade-offs, and rely on unblockable/evasive effects that bypass counters once resolved.
Race them with faster threats, use early interaction to disrupt their mana, and sideboard in hate for their key ramp spells.
Win the resource war by trading efficiently, sideboard in hate for Utopia Sprawl, and prioritize value plays over aggressive overextensions.

common_threats: Watch for removal spells, board wipes, and counter magic.
Note when opponents save removal for your key threats.
Track their mana development to anticipate when they can answer your plays.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Gruul Ramp archetype and signature cards
